dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3405 IS: PRC Financial Intermediary Review Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. McCormick (for himself and Mr. Fetterman) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs

form.official-title: To require the Securities and Exchange Commission to conduct a study regarding certain brokers and dealers that are controlled by or organized under the laws of the People’s Republic of China, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the PRC Financial Intermediary Review Act.
Not later than 1 year after the date of the enactment of this Act, the Securities and Exchange Commission shall—

paragraph.text: conduct a study on the transparency of, and cooperation regarding—

clause.text: members of a national securities association and registered with the Securities and Exchange Commission; and
controlled by or organized under the laws of the People’s Republic of China; and

subparagraph.text: investment advisers that are registered with the Securities and Exchange Commission and controlled by or organized under the laws of the People’s Republic of China; and

paragraph.text: submit to Congress a report that includes the results of the study conducted under paragraph (1).
